title: Sri Lalith Kumar Jain vs M/S Hdfc Bank Limited on 7 December, 2018
body: Sri Lalith Kumar Jain vs M/S Hdfc Bank Limited on 7 December, 2018 Author: S.G.Pandit Bench: S.G. Pandit 1 IN THE HIGH COURT OF KARNATAKA AT BENGALURU DATED THIS THE 7TH DAY OF DECEMBER, 2018 BEFORE THE HON' BLE MR. JUSTICE S.G. PANDIT WRIT PETITION No.43810 OF 2016 (GM- CPC ) BETWEEN: Sri. Lalith Kumar Jain, S/o. Late Mittalal Jain, Aged about 46 years, Residing at Door No.2909, Kantharaj Urs Road, Saraswathipuram, Mysuru City - 570 009. ... Petitioner (By Sri.S.Rajashekar, Advocate) AND: M/s.HDFC Bank Limited, No.2940, D-8, Dwetha Complex, Temple Road, V.V.Puram, Vontikoppal, Mysuru City - 570 002. Represented by Sri.Siddalingaiah, S/o. Sri. Kataginath. ...Respondent (By Sri.Francis Xavier, Advocate) ******** 2 This writ petition is filed under Article 227 of the Constitution of India praying to quash the order dated 05.07.2016 passed on I.A.No.2 by the Principal District Judge at Mysuru in R.A.No.146/2015 vide Annex-A and etc., This writ petition coming on for orders this day, the Court made the flowing:- ORDER The petitioner is before this Court assailing the order dated 05.07.2016 on I.A.No.II in RA.No.146/2015 on the file of Principal District Judge, Mysuru. 2. Even though the matter is listed for orders, with consent of the parties, the same as taken for final disposal. 3. The petitioner is defendant and respondent is plaintiff in O.S.No.270/2011 on the file of III Additional Senior Civil Judge and CJM, Mysuru. The suit is one for recovery of a sum of Rs.6,01,947/-. The suit came to be decreed by judgment and decree dated 3 06.03.2015. The order portion of the judgment, which reads as follows: "The suit of the plaintiff is decreed with costs. The defendant is hereby directed to repay the amount of Rs.6,01,947/- (Rupees Six Lakhs One Thousand Nine hundred and Forty Seven only) to the plaintiff with interest at the rate of 24% p.a. from the date of suit till its realization". 4. The petitioner-defendant aggrieved by the judgment and decree dated 06.03.2015 filed an appeal in RA.No.146/2015 before Principal District Judge, Mysuru. The respondent-plaintiff in the appeal filed I.A.No.II under Section 49 Explanation 3 of Karnataka Court Fee and Suit Valuation Act, 1958 (hereinafter called as 'Act' for short) seeking for direction to the appellant-petitioner herein to pay additional court fee. The trial Court by order dated 05.07.2016 allowed the application and directed the petitioner-appellant to pay 4 court fee in accordance with Section 49 Explanation 3 of the Act by calculating court fee on the amount of Rs.6,01,947/- together with interest at 24% p.a. from the date of suit till the date of pronouncement of judgment and to pay deficit court fee. 5. Heard the learned counsel for the petitioner and learned counsel for respondent and perused the writ papers. 6. The learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the Court below is not justified in directing the petitioner to pay the deficit court fee especially when the subject matter of appeal filed by the petitioner is challenge to the imposition of interest. Further, it is submitted that the plaintiff bifurcated the principal amount and the interest amount payable by defendant at the time of institution of the suit. Therefore, he is not liable to pay court fee of the interest portion of the claim amount. 5 7. Per contra, the learned counsel for the respondent-bank submits that as per Section 49 Explanation 3 of the Act, the appellant is liable to pay court fee on the interest portion also from the date of suit till the date of pronouncement of judgment. He relies upon the decision in the case of Andhra Bank Vs. Sunanda Industrial Chemicals (P) Ltd. , reported in I.L.R.1996 KAR 183 in support of his contention. 8. The suit is filed for recovery of a sum of Rs.6,01,947/-. The suit is decreed directing the defendant to pay a sum of Rs.6,01,947/- with interest at 24% p.a. from the date of suit till its realization. The petitioner-appellant filed I.A. in RA.No.146/2015 seeking for a direction that on appeal memorandum, the appellant is liable to pay court fee under Section 49 Explanation 3 of the Act, which reads as follows: 6 " Section 49 :- in an appeal shall be the same as the fee that would be payable in the Court of first instance on the subject matter of the appeal. Provided that, in levying fee on a memorandum of appeal against a final decree by a person whose appeal against the preliminary decree passed by the Court of first instance or by the Court of appeal is pending, credit shall be given for the fee paid by such person in the appeal against the preliminary decree. Explanation No.1 ................... Explanation No.2 ..................... Explanation No.3:- In claims which include the award of interest subsequent to the institution of the suit the interest accrued during the pendency of the suit till the date of decree shall be deemed to be part of the subject matter of the appeal except where such interest is relinquished". Explanation No.4 ....................... Explanation No.5 ......................." 7 9. It is clear from the above provision that when the claim includes the award of interest, the interest accrued during the pendency of the suit till date of decree shall be deemed to be part of the subject matter of the decree. Therefore, the trial Court has rightly directed the petitioner-appellant herein to calculate court fee on the amount of Rs.6,01,947/- with interest at 24% p.a. from the date of filing of suit till the date of filing of the appeal. 10. In the decision relied upon by the learned counsel for respondent-bank in the case of Andhra Bank Vs. Sunanda Industrial Chemicals (P) Ltd. , reported in I.L.R.1996 KAR 183, this Court at Para No.2 has held as under: "The contention of Sri. G.S.Venkatachalapathy, learned Counsel for the appellant is that since no Court Fee is payable in respect of interest pendente lite the Appeal has to be valued only in 8 accordance with the provisions of Section 47 (ii) of the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuations Act. That would be Rs.200/- only, which has been paid on this Appeal. We do not agree with this contention in view of the clear provisions of Explanation (3) to Section 49 of the Act, which reads thus:- "Explanation No.3:- In claims which include the award interest subsequent to the institution of the suit the interest accrued during the pendency of the suit till the date of decree shall be deemed to be part of the subject matter of the appeal except where such interest is relinquished". It is clear that the emphasis is on 'any claim' including the award of interest which had accrued during the pendency of the suit, regardless whether it is allowed or refused; nonetheless, it is deemed to be part of the subject matter of the Appeal. If any such claim is agitated in any appeal against the original decree, Court fee has to be paid on the value of such claim". 9 11. In view of the above principles, I find no error or illegality in the order dated 05.07.2016 on I.A.No.II filed under Section 49 Explanation 3 of the Act in R.A.No.146/2015 by the appellate Court. No ground is made out to interfere with the order passed by the appellate Court. Accordingly, writ petition is disposed of. Sd/- JUDGE KTY
